title: Heat exchange panel, solar thermal system and associated methods
abstract: There is disclosed a structure in the form heat exchange panel (30) adapted to be fittable to or proximate a rear surface (19) of a photovoltaic panel (10) having a frame (12). The heat exchange panel (30) may include a panel body (32) formed substantially of a heat conductive material, an attachment arrangement (34) supported by the panel body (32), and a conduit (36) arranged to contact the panel body (32) so as to exchange heat between the panel body (32) and a fluid carried by the conduit (36). Examples of the attachment arrangement (34) may include at least one adjustable member (64) adapted to provide at least one of widthwise and lengthwise adjustability and a coupling (68) adapted to non-invasively engage the frame (12) of the photovoltaic panel (10) so as to allow the heat exchange panel (30) to be retained thereagainst in the fitted condition. Further examples of structure, an associated system and methods are also disclosed.
claims:
Claims The claims defining the Invention are as follows: 1. A heat exchange panel adapted to be fittable to a rear surface of a photovoltaic panel having a frame in a fitted condition, the heat exchange panel including: a panel body formed substantially of a heat conductive material, an attachment arrangement supported by the panel body, and a conduit arranged to contact the panel body so as to exchange heat between the panel body and a fluid carried by the conduit, wherein the attachment arrangement includes at least one adjustable member adapted to provide at least one of widthwise and lengthwise adjustability and a coupling adapted to non-invasively engage the frame of the photovoltaic panel so as to retain the panel body against the rear surface in the fitted condition. 2. The heat exchange panel according to claim 1, wherein the panel body includes a plurality of tabs arranged to be foldable at least partially over the conduit so as to retain the conduit against the panel body thereby allowing heat exchange therebetween. 3. A heat exchange panel adapted to be fittable to a rear surface of a photovoltaic panel having a frame in a fitted condition, the heat exchange panel including: a panel body formed substantially of a heat conductive material, an attachment arrangement supported by the panel body adapted to couple the panel body to the frame in the fitted condition, and a conduit arranged to contact the panel body so as to exchange heat between the panel body and a fluid carried by the conduit, wherein the panel body includes a plurality of tabs arranged to be foldable at least partially over the conduit so as to retain the conduit against the panel body thereby allowing heat exchange therebetween. 4. The heat exchange panel according to claim 2, wherein the attachment arrangement includes at least one adjustable member adapted to provide at least one of widthwise and lengthwise adjustability and a coupling adapted to non-invasively engage the frame of the photovoltaic panel so as to allow the heat exchange panel to be retained thereto in the fitted condition. 5. The heat exchange panel according to claim 2 or claim 4, wherein the coupling is arranged to connect the frame via a friction fit. 6. The heat exchange panel according to claim 5, wherein the coupling is arranged to clamp to the frame. 7. The heat exchange panel according to claim 6, wherein the coupling includes a hand operable cam and a opposing flange between which the frame is captured, the hand operable cam being operative to clamp the frame and including a locking means to maintain the clamp in a clamped condition. 8. The heat exchange panel according to claim 7, wherein the hand operable cam has a substantially knife shaped body including a handle and a curved cam portion that engages with the frame in the fitted condition, the knife shaped body being pivotally coupled to the at least one adjustable member between the handle and the curved cam portion by a fastener. 9. The heat exchange panel according to claim 2 or claim 4, wherein the tabs are provided in the form of opposing fingers spaced apart to receive the conduit and having a length to wrap at least half way around the conduit in a folded condition. 10. The heat exchange panel according to claim 9, wherein the opposing fingers are provided in spaced apart groups having at least two pairs of opposing fingers. 11. The heat exchange panel according to claim 10, wherein the spaced apart groups are arranged such that the conduit zig-zags between opposing sides of the panel body. 12. The heat exchange panel according to any one of claims 5 to 11, wherein the panel body is formed from a metal sheet and the tabs are initially formed from cuts and upturned from the metal sheet. 13. The heat exchange panel according to claim 11, wherein the panel body 

 includes at least one further cut out arranged at least one or receive or provide clearance for a junction box at a rear of the PV panel. 14. The heat exchange panel according to claim 12 or claim 13, wherein the panel body includes a further cut out to provide a upturned support tab by which the at least one adjustable member is supported. 15. The heat exchange panel according to any one of claims 5 to 14, wherein the at least one adjustable member is provided in the form of adjustable members that are adjustably secured to one another in the fitted condition by a further locking means. 16. A hybrid photovoltaic panel arrangement including a photovoltaic panel having a frame and a heat exchange panel according to any one of claims 1 to 15 fitted to a rear surface of the photovoltaic panel. 17. A heat exchange system for a photovoltaic panel, the system including a plurality hybrid photovoltaic panel arrangements according to claim 16 in which the conduits are in fluid communication with one another. 18. A heat exchange system for a photovoltaic panel, the system including a heat exchange panel according to any one of claim 1 to 15 fitted with the photovoltaic panel in the fitted condition and a heat pump, the heat pump being in fluid communication with the conduits. 19. A heat exchange system for a photovoltaic panel, the system including a plurality of heat exchange panels according to any one of claim 1 to 15 fitted with respective photovoltaic panels in the fitted condition and a heat pump system in fluid communication with the plurality of heat exchange panels, the heat pump being connected to new or existing hot water or central heating systems, which is powered by the solar photovoltaic panel, to which the heat exchange panels are mounted. 20. A method of forming of a heat exchange panel for a photovoltaic panel, the method including: 

 providing a unitary metal sheet adapted to fit with a rear surface of the photovoltaic panel; forming a conduit arranged to substantially snake between opposing sides of the metal sheet; forming, by cutting the metal sheet, a series of outward extending fingers having a space there-between to receive the conduit, bending the fingers about the conduit so as to least partially extend around and retain the conduit, forming, by cutting the metal sheet, an outward extending support tab; attaching a length or width adjustable attachment arrangement to the support tab; and providing a coupling, at or toward opposing ends of the attachment arrangement, adapted to non-invasively engage the frame of the photovoltaic panel so as to allow the heat exchange panel to be retained thereto in a fitted condition. 21. A method of installation heat exchange panel with a photovoltaic panel, the method including: placing a substantially flat face of a panel body of the heat exchange panel against a rear face of the photovoltaic panel with cut outs of the panel body providing clearance for a junction box of the photovoltaic panel and with an opposing face of the panel body having conduits secured by bendable tabs facing away from the photovoltaic panel; and adjusting at least one adjustable member of an attachment arrangement supported by the panel body such that couplings carried at opposing ends of the at least one adjustable member are positioned to non-invasively engage the frame of the photovoltaic panel; and actuating the couplings to secure the heat exchange panel to the photovoltaic panel in a fitted condition.
